Treat any production address not linked from these docs as unverified. See [Polaris Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status) for the current network state.

## How the system deploys

Polaris is a *factory* of pAsset instances. A single **shared core** — the bonding curve, POLAR, the Fee Router, and the oracles — deploys **once**. Then **one instance set** deploys **per pAsset** (pUSD, pGOLD, …): each gets its own CDP manager, Stability Pool, and internal Peg Stability Module, but all share the same pETH collateral, bonding curve, and POLAR.

So the address table has a **core section** plus **one block per pAsset**. The current docs focus on **pUSD** and **pGOLD**; production deployment status remains governed by [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status), while current Sepolia artifacts are listed on [Public Testnet 1](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet).

## Current Public Testnet 1 (Sepolia)

Public Testnet 1 runs on **Sepolia (chain ID `11155111`)**. Its addresses are testnet artifacts, may be redeployed, and use testnet stand-ins such as mock WETH, mock oracles, and testnet price feeds. Do not use them as production defaults.

The manifest paths below are relative to `/home/ahirice/Documents/git/polaris`.

Source | Use it for | Location

[Public Testnet 1 contracts](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet#public-testnet-1-contracts) | Current Sepolia addresses in a user-facing table | Docs
[Public Testnet 1 parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet#public-testnet-1-parameters) | Current Sepolia MCR, Emergency Mode thresholds, curve constants, and fee defaults | Docs
Core manifest | Current Sepolia core addresses and curve constants | `deployments/sepolia/deployment-manifest.core.ts`
pUSD manifest | Current Sepolia pUSD instance addresses and manifest parameters | `deployments/sepolia/deployment-manifest.pusd.ts`
pGOLD manifest | Current Sepolia pGOLD instance addresses and manifest parameters | `deployments/sepolia/deployment-manifest.pgold.ts`
Deploy script defaults | Fee-floor, alpha, decay, and other defaults not all written into the manifests | `script/DeployPolaris.s.sol`

For testnet work, load the manifest for the network and pAsset you are actually using. For production work, wait until this page publishes canonical mainnet addresses.

## Shared core

Contract | Source (`src/`) | Address

**pETH** — the [bonding curve](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth): swaps ETH ↔ pETH, enforces the rising floor. pETH is the sole collateral and is this ERC-20 itself. | `BondingCurve/ABDKBondingCurve.sol` | _Forthcoming_
Bonding-curve token splitter — internal pETH accounting (floor / variable components). | `BondingCurve/BondingCurveTokenSplitter.sol`, `BondingCurve/VPToken.sol` | _Forthcoming_
**POLAR** — the stewardship token. The [conversion auction](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/conversion-auctions) (burn pETH → mint POLAR) lives **inside this contract**, not a separate deployment. | `Shares/ShareSystem.sol` | _Forthcoming_
POLAR staking — stake POLAR to receive routed yield. The [vePOLAR](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship/vepolar) lock layer is forthcoming; today this is the staking surface. | _Forthcoming_ | _Forthcoming_
[Fee Router](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship/fee-router) — buffers pETH fee revenue and routes rewards to troves and staking. | `FeeRouter.sol` | _Forthcoming_
Stewardship — bounded-parameter control; cannot reach the immutable core. | _Forthcoming_ | _Forthcoming_
Reserve token (WETH) — the wrapped ETH the bonding curve takes in. | external WETH | _Forthcoming_
Reserve Loans — borrow ETH against pETH at the floor. | `ReserveLoansManager.sol`, `ReserveLoansNFT.sol` | _Forthcoming_

## Per-pAsset instance

One set deploys per pAsset. Key everything by `(pAsset, owner)` — a pUSD trove and a pGOLD trove are unrelated positions in different instances.

Contract | Source (`src/`) | Address

**pAsset token** (e.g. pUSD, pGOLD) — the synthetic pegged ERC-20. | `StableToken.sol` | _Forthcoming_
**CDP / Trove manager** — trove accounting, mint/redeem, liquidations, interest, Emergency Mode. | `CDPManager.sol` | _Forthcoming_
Trove NFT — tokenizes each trove position. | `CDPNFT.sol` | _Forthcoming_
[Stability Pool](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield) — absorbs liquidations; the primary yield venue. | `StabilityPool.sol` | _Forthcoming_
Collateral Surplus Pool — holds post-liquidation collateral surplus for borrowers to claim. | `CollSurplusPool.sol` | _Forthcoming_
**Internal Peg Stability Module** — mint/redeem the pAsset against pETH; the peg and [redemption](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations) entry point. This is not a custodial or external-collateral PSM. | `PegStabilityModule.sol` | _Forthcoming_
Interest controller — primary (autonomous) and secondary (utilization) interest rates. | `InterestRate/InterestController.sol` | _Forthcoming_
Primary interest router — splits borrower interest across the Stability Pool, POLAR staking, and ecosystem. | `PrimaryInterestRouter.sol` | _Forthcoming_
Price feed — the pAsset's reference price path. | `PUSDTestnetPriceFeed.sol` / `PGOLDTestnetPriceFeed.sol` | _Forthcoming_
Oracle (medianiser) — median of multiple ETH/USD feeds (plus XAU/USD for pGOLD). | `Medianiser.sol` | _Forthcoming_

> **Read helpers** (`Helpers/MultiCDPGetter.sol`, `Helpers/MultiReserveLoanGetter.sol`) are view contracts for batched reads; integrations may use them but should treat direct contract reads as the source of truth.

> **A note on naming.** "pETH" and "the bonding curve" are the **same contract**. The "conversion auction" is **part of POLAR**, not a separate contract. The per-pAsset internal Peg Stability Module is the pETH mint/redeem path, not the custodial/external-collateral PSM model Polaris rejects. The "vePOLAR" lock layer is **not yet production-deployed**; production yield and staking details are pending. `pCHF`, `pBigMac`, and other catalog entries are illustrative; **pUSD** and **pGOLD** are the current Public Testnet 1 focus.

## Before you interact

1. Confirm the **network and chain ID** match the official deployment manifest.
2. Confirm every **address** matches [Public Testnet 1](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet) for Sepolia or this page for production once populated.
3. Confirm the **ABI** matches that verified source.
4. Never paste an address from a screenshot, DM, or search result without that explorer check — address-spoofing is how these attacks succeed.

While production addresses are pending, see [Audits & Security](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/audits-security) for the security model, [Public Testnet 1](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet) for Sepolia artifacts, or the [Glossary](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources) for what each contract does.

Relevant app/search vocabulary: app, official app, testnet, Sepolia, WETH, WETH faucet, Zap, Split, Swap, Borrow, Earn, Guide, Advanced, Analytics, APR, Reserve Loan.

---

## Polaris Finance FAQ

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/faq

# Polaris Finance FAQ

Short answers, grouped by theme. Each links to the page that covers it in full.

The official public Public Testnet 1 app is live at [app.testnet.polarisfinance.io](https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/) on Sepolia only. Production/mainnet app status, canonical production contracts, final audit reports, and the disclosure program are not published yet. Check [Polaris Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status), [Public Testnet 1](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet), and [Safety and Verification](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/safety-verification) before interacting with any frontend or address.

## General

### What is Polaris?

A self-scaling stablecoin operating system: trustless, immutable infrastructure for minting synthetic assets (pAssets) backed entirely by onchain collateral and yield. No T-bills, no CEXs. See [Why Polaris?](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/why-polaris)

### What is a pAsset?

A synthetic debt asset minted from a Polaris CDP, each overcollateralized by pETH and counterparty-free. pUSD and pGOLD are Testnet-active; pCHF, pBigMac, and beyond are illustrative or future catalog examples until launch status says otherwise. pETH itself is separate: it is the bonding-curve collateral token. See [The pAsset Catalog](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/passet-catalog).

### What is pUSD?

The flagship pAsset, designed to track the US dollar and be backed by pETH. It sits in the same counterparty-free design family as LUSD and BOLD, adding Polaris's pETH collateral and yield mechanics. See [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting).

### What is pETH?

ETH deposited into a two-way [bonding curve](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth). It is the only collateral Polaris accepts, is yield-bearing, and has a [floor price](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price) that only rises.

### What is POLAR?

The stewardship token and the protocol's outward-facing growth lever. After the initial distribution, new POLAR can be minted only by burning pETH in a [conversion auction](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/conversion-auctions). See [The POLAR Token](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar).

### Which chain does Polaris run on?

The current public phase is Public Testnet 1 on Sepolia (chain ID 11155111 ). The official testnet app is [app.testnet.polarisfinance.io](https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/), and current Sepolia artifacts are listed on [Public Testnet 1](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet). Production is intended for Ethereum mainnet (chain ID 1 ) once launched, but the production app and mainnet contracts are not live or published yet.

## Minting and troves

### What is a trove?

Your individual borrowing position in a Polaris CDP, isolated with its own collateral, debt, and collateralization ratio. Polaris uses trove, never "vault". See [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting).

### How do I mint a pAsset?

You open a trove: post pETH as collateral and mint the pAsset directly from the protocol, owing that debt back. You are your own mini central bank, not borrowing another user's funds. See [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting).

### Why does Polaris accept only pETH as collateral?

External collateral like staked-ETH tokens would be a dependency that caps scaling and reintroduces counterparty risk. pETH is a yield-bearing ETH proxy with a rising floor, which dampens the CDP model's capital-inefficiency weakness. See [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting).

### How are interest rates set?

Autonomously, with no governance vote and no price oracle for the peg. Redemption volume (profitable below peg) and minting volume (profitable above peg) are read as onchain signals of where the peg sits, and rates adjust accordingly. See [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting).

## pETH

### Is pETH the same as ETH?

No. pETH is ETH inside the bonding curve. It is volatile against ETH but has a contract-defined lower bound under the [floor-price](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price) assumptions, and it earns yield. Think of it as stETH with different trade-offs: contract-native liquidity and an onchain exit path, but volatility against ETH and smart-contract/execution risk.

## Yield

### Where does the yield come from?

From onchain activity, not offchain T-bills: CDP interest, bonding-curve swap fees, conversion gains, and PolarEX swap fees, all of which grow with usage. See [The Stability Pool](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield).

## Stewardship

### Is there governance?

No unbounded governance. The core is immutable; stewards steward only quantitative parameters, many within hardcoded ranges. We call it stewardship because stewards cannot alter the core; they cannot govern it. See [Stewardship, not Governance](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship).

## Security

### Can I lose funds?

Yes. Polaris carries smart-contract risk, collateral price risk, liquidation risk, and Stability Pool first-loss risk, among others. Trustlessness means no one can step in to make you whole. Read the full [Risk Disclosure](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/risk-disclosure) before using the protocol.

### Is Polaris audited?

Final audit reports are not published in these docs yet. Until they appear on [Audits & Security](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/audits-security), treat the protocol as unaudited.

### Where are contract addresses listed?

Current Sepolia testnet addresses are listed on [Public Testnet 1](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet) and in the linked protocol manifests. Canonical production addresses are not published yet. Once deployed, they will be listed with network, chain ID, and source-verification links on [Contracts & Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts). Any claimed production address found elsewhere before then is unverified.

Next steps:
- [Glossary](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources): Look up any Polaris term.
- [Core Concepts](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/core-concepts): The one-page map of the entire system.
- [Polaris Blog](https://polarisfinance.io/blog/): Long-form essays and deep dives.

Relevant app/search vocabulary: app, official app, testnet, Sepolia, WETH, WETH faucet, Zap, Split, Swap, Borrow, Earn, Guide, Advanced, Analytics, APR, Reserve Loan.

---

## Polaris Risk Disclosure

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/risk-disclosure

# Polaris Risk Disclosure

Polaris is trustless, immutable infrastructure. That design choice has real costs, and we disclose them before we claim the benefits. Every risk below is paired with the mechanism that mitigates it (*mitigates*, not eliminates). Read this page in full before you mint, deposit, or lock anything.

> **Note:** Nothing here is financial advice. Polaris is in public Public Testnet 1 on Sepolia: the official testnet app is [app.testnet.polarisfinance.io](https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/), and testnet assets have no monetary value. Production/mainnet app status, canonical production contracts, final audit reports, and the disclosure program are not published yet. Trustlessness means the protocol will not, and cannot, step in to make you whole. You are responsible for understanding the position you take.

## Smart-contract risk

Polaris lives entirely onchain. Its bonding curve, CDPs, Stability Pool, and conversion auctions are smart contracts, and any smart contract can contain bugs.

**Mitigation.** The intended core is immutable and admin-key-free, removing upgrade and governance-key attack surface but raising the stakes on getting the code right. Final audit reports are not published in these docs yet; until reports appear on [Audits & Security](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/audits-security), treat the protocol as unaudited. Immutability cuts both ways: it cannot be tampered with, and it cannot be patched. Residual risk is never zero.

## Collateral and price risk

The only collateral is [pETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth), which is **volatile against ETH**. pETH trades at a varying premium above its [floor price](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price), and that premium can compress sharply in a drawdown.

**Mitigation.** The [floor ratio](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price) frames your curve-implied downside in ETH terms before execution and technical risk: a 90% floor ratio means 10% drawdown to the floor, a 67% floor ratio means 33% drawdown. Your risk is set at *entry*: the spread between your entry price and the floor. Under the intended curve invariant, the floor rises during normal operation; it is still dependent on functioning contracts and live Ethereum execution.

> **Warning:** A high floor ratio does not protect against ETH's own volatility. pETH's floor is denominated in ETH terms. If ETH itself falls against the dollar, your pETH falls with it.

## Oracle risk

Pricing pETH collateral for liquidation requires an ETH price feed. A stale, manipulated, or failed oracle could misprice collateral and trigger wrong liquidations, or fail to trigger correct ones.

**Mitigation.** Peg awareness needs no oracle: redemption and minting volume act as the protocol's internal price signal for each pAsset (see [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting)). The oracle dependency is meant to be confined to pricing the *collateral*, ETH. Current Sepolia testnet price-feed and medianiser addresses are listed on [Public Testnet 1](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet); final production providers and feed addresses are forthcoming. Do not treat any production oracle address as official until it is listed in [Contracts & Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts).

## Peg and depeg risk

A pAsset can trade away from its peg, below it in a panic or above it in a demand spike. Depegs can be temporary or, under severe stress, sustained.

**Mitigation.** Two opposing arbitrage mechanisms defend the peg structurally:

- **[Redemptions](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations)** make it profitable to buy the pAsset below peg and redeem it for collateral, pushing the price up toward \$1.
- **Minting** makes it profitable to mint and sell the pAsset above peg, pushing the price back down.

Because both pay arbitrageurs the moment the peg slips, they activate without anyone's permission. pUSD is a counterparty-free stablecoin in the same trust-minimized family as LUSD and BOLD, so the peg is not propped up by a redeemable offchain reserve; it is held by onchain incentives.

## Liquidation risk (borrowers)

If your [trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting)'s [Individual Collateralization Ratio (ICR)](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources) falls below the minimum, the trove is [liquidated](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations/liquidations): you lose your collateral to cover the debt.

**Mitigation.** Liquidation logic is transparent and auditable onchain, with no discretionary calls. You control your ICR: post more collateral, or repay debt, to stay clear of the threshold. pETH's rising floor and yield-bearing nature dampen, but do not remove, the volatility that causes liquidations. Monitor your trove actively, especially in volatile markets.

## Stability Pool first-loss risk (depositors)

Depositing pAssets into the [Stability Pool](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield) is the primary way to earn yield, but the Pool is the system's **first-loss buffer**. When a trove is liquidated, your deposited pAssets are burned to cancel its debt, and you receive the liquidated pETH collateral in exchange.

**Mitigation.** Liquidations are designed to occur while the trove is still overcollateralized, so depositors typically receive collateral worth *more* than the pAssets they gave up, and that surplus is the source of the Pool's yield. The risk is the opposite case: a liquidation processed below the value of the burned deposit, or a sharp collapse in the received collateral's value. You take on liquidation-processing risk in exchange for yield; size your deposit accordingly.

## Recovery Mode

When the system-wide reserve backing relative to total debt (the [reserve-to-debt ratio, shown as the TCR](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources)) drops below a threshold, the protocol enters [Recovery Mode](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations/recovery-mode), a defensive state with tighter requirements.

**Mitigation.** Recovery Mode is a feature, not a failure: it raises the bar for the whole system to rebuild collateralization quickly. Borrowers bear the cost: in Recovery Mode you cannot withdraw collateral or take on more debt unless you clear a higher minimum ratio and do not lower your ICR (pure collateral top-ups and repayments stay allowed). Liquidation eligibility itself does not change; it stays at the normal minimum in every mode. Keep your ICR comfortably above the minimum so a stress event does not box in your ability to act.

## Stewardship and economic risk

Stewards control quantitative parameters: the interest splits, conversion-price parameters, and the [converter rebate](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources). Parameter changes can affect your yield and your position's economics.

**Mitigation.** Stewardship is *constrained*: many economic parameters move only within hardcoded ranges (for example the converter rebate and the interest-split floors), and the core logic cannot be touched at all (see [Stewardship, not Governance](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship)). Some conversion-price parameters are not yet bounded in the current prototype; instead, conversion issuance is self-throttled by its [spike-and-decay pricing](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/conversion-auctions), so large or rapid minting limits itself. What remains is ordinary parameter risk, not the unbounded-governance risk that has denatured other protocols. Final per-parameter bounds are pending audited deployment.

## MEV risk

Redemptions, liquidations, and [conversion auctions](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/conversion-auctions) are profitable, permissionless actions, so they attract MEV searchers and arbitrage bots. Ordinary users competing by hand will usually lose the race to automation.

**Mitigation.** This is by design: MEV competition keeps the peg tight and the floor rising, because searchers do the protocol's stabilizing work for profit. Do not expect to win arbitrage races manually; interact with the protocol for the position you want (a trove, a Stability Pool deposit, a pETH hold), not to front-run the bots.

## Dependency risk (Ethereum)

Polaris is currently in public Public Testnet 1 on Sepolia, and production is intended for Ethereum mainnet (chain ID `1`) once launched. Production Polaris inherits Ethereum's risks: consensus failures, severe congestion, or a contentious fork.

**Mitigation.** This dependency is deliberate and unhedged: the only counterparties Polaris is meant to ask you to trust are immutable code and Ethereum itself. We do not spread across chains to dilute that exposure, because doing so would reintroduce the bridge and external-dependency risks Polaris exists to avoid.

## Frontend and phishing risk

Fake frontends, fake contract addresses, and fake support channels can appear before users know what the official ones look like. A malicious frontend can ask you to approve tokens, sign dangerous messages, or connect to the wrong network.

**Mitigation.** For Public Testnet 1, use only [https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/](https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/) on Sepolia (`11155111`) and only with no-value test assets. Use no production app URL, production network, production contract address, audit claim, or bug-bounty link unless it is listed in these docs. Never enter a seed phrase, private key, or recovery words into any Polaris-related site. The current status checklist lives on [Polaris Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status), [Public Testnet 1](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet), and [Safety and Verification](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/safety-verification).

---

For the reasoning behind these trade-offs, read [Why Polaris?](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/why-polaris). For the precise definition of any term above, see the [Glossary](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources).

---

## Polaris Safety and Verification

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/safety-verification

# Polaris Safety and Verification

**Who this is for:** every Polaris user and integrator; run this path before connecting a wallet, sending ETH, minting a pAsset, depositing into a pool, locking POLAR, or integrating contracts.

**Prerequisites:** none. Complete this path *first*, before any other journey, because impersonation and phishing are most likely around launch and testnet phases.

> **Launch status:** [Polaris Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status) is the source of truth. As of June 8, 2026, public Public Testnet 1 is on Sepolia (`11155111`) and the official testnet app is [app.testnet.polarisfinance.io](https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/). Production Ethereum mainnet (`1`) is forthcoming, and no production app, production addresses, ABIs, verified source, final parameters, or audit reports are published yet.

> **Open Testnet App:** Use [https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/](https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/) only on Sepolia (`11155111`) with no-value test assets. Treat any production frontend, token, contract, airdrop, or support message as unverified until it is linked from [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status).

App anchors: **Dashboard** first for network and system state, then any action tab's pre-confirmation screen for contract, token, amount, fee, approval, and simulation checks.

## Verification checklist

Work top to bottom; each row names a check and where it will be confirmed at launch.

Check | Where it will be confirmed

Current launch phase and network | [Polaris Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status)
Testnet app URL and production app status | [Polaris Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status)
Deployed addresses | [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status), official Sepolia manifests, and [Contracts and Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts) once production is live
Verified source and ABIs | [Contracts and Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts), official manifests, and block explorer links
Audit reports | [Audits and Security](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/audits-security)
Final user parameters | The relevant product page and contract reference
Stewardship bounds | [Stewardship, not Governance](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship)

## Things Polaris will not ask for

- A seed phrase or private key.
- KYC for protocol use.
- A direct transfer to a support wallet.
- Interaction with an unpublished address.
- Trust in a frontend that is not linked from official Polaris channels.

## Risk docs

Read [Risk Disclosure](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/risk-disclosure) for smart-contract, collateral, liquidation, peg, Stability Pool, pETH, stewardship, and phishing risks. Read [Audits and Security](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/audits-security) for the security model and audit status.

## Developer verification

For apps and indexers, add explicit allowlists for network IDs and contract addresses. Testnet allowlists should point to Sepolia (`11155111`) artifacts from [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status) and the linked manifests. Production allowlists should stay empty until production addresses are published. Your integration should fail closed if the deployment manifest, ABI, or verified source does not match the official docs.

---

## Polaris Public Testnet 1 Source of Truth

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet

# Polaris Public Testnet 1 Source of Truth

This page is the source of truth for the current public Polaris testnet environment. It is not a production launch page.

> **Last verified:** June 8, 2026. Public Testnet 1 is public on Sepolia. Production mainnet is not live.

> **Open Testnet App:** [app.testnet.polarisfinance.io](https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/) is the official Public Testnet 1 app. Use it only on Sepolia (`11155111`) with test assets that have no monetary value.

## Public Testnet 1 status

Item | Public Testnet 1 source of truth

Public phase | **Public Testnet 1**
Network | **Sepolia**
Chain ID | `11155111`
Official testnet app | [https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/](https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/)
Production app | Not live or published in these docs
Production contracts | Not live or published in these docs
Testnet contracts | Replaceable Sepolia artifacts from the manifests listed below
Testnet value | No real value, no production redemption, no mainnet claim
Testnet gas | Sepolia ETH from a Sepolia faucet
Testnet reserve token | Mock WETH/reserve token minted from the app faucet when that faucet is enabled for your wallet
Testnet-active pAssets | pUSD and pGOLD
Illustrative or future pAssets | pCHF, pBigMac, and other catalog examples

## First testnet flow

1. Get a small amount of Sepolia ETH from a public Sepolia faucet for gas.
2. Open [app.testnet.polarisfinance.io](https://app.testnet.polarisfinance.io/) and connect a wallet on Sepolia (`11155111`).
3. Use the app's reserve-token faucet if it is enabled for your wallet. The deployment manifest names the reserve token `WETH`; the prototype app may display it as ETH for readability. It is testnet-only and has no value.
4. Use the bonding-curve / swap surface to exchange test reserve tokens for pETH.
5. Borrow or mint a Testnet-active pAsset such as pUSD or pGOLD where the app surface supports it.
6. Optionally deposit the pAsset into the Stability Pool to test the Earn flow.

If the app faucet is disabled for your wallet, do not send mainnet ETH or mainnet WETH. Wait for the published testnet faucet path or use only the assets explicitly supported by the app.

## Manifest locations

The current Sepolia artifacts are checked into the Polaris protocol repository at `/home/ahirice/Documents/git/polaris`:

Manifest | What it covers | Protocol repo path

Core manifest | Reserve token, pETH/bonding curve, POLAR/share system, staking, fee router, reserve loans, stewardship, splitter, vpToken | `deployments/sepolia/deployment-manifest.core.ts`
pUSD manifest | pUSD token, CDP manager, Stability Pool, PSM, price feed, medianiser, interest controller, read helper | `deployments/sepolia/deployment-manifest.pusd.ts`
pGOLD manifest | pGOLD token, CDP manager, Stability Pool, PSM, price feed, ETH/USD and XAU/USD medianisers, interest controller, read helper | `deployments/sepolia/deployment-manifest.pgold.ts`
Deploy script defaults | Fee floors, fee alpha values, decay factors, initial rates, and deployment constants not all written into the manifests | `script/DeployPolaris.s.sol`

Treat these as testnet artifacts. They are useful for testing, indexer scaffolding, and UI verification, but they are not production-canonical.

These are Sepolia addresses from the current manifests. They may be redeployed and should not be hardcoded as production defaults.

## Public Testnet 1 parameters

Values marked "manifest" are copied from the current Sepolia manifests. Values marked "deploy script" come from the current deployment script defaults; verify live quotes in the app or contract before relying on them for a transaction.

### Shared core parameters

Parameter | Public Testnet 1 value | Source

Bonding curve alpha | `193218996973422650` | Core manifest
Bonding curve beta | `150000000000000000` (15%) | Core manifest

### pAsset instance parameters

Parameter | pUSD | pGOLD | Source

Minimum collateral ratio (MCR) | 115% | 115% | Manifest
Emergency Mode action MCR | 150% | 150% | Manifest
Emergency Mode trigger ratio | 110% | 110% | Manifest
Mint fee floor | 0.05% | 0.05% | Deploy script
Redemption fee floor | 0.05% | 0.05% | Deploy script
Mint alpha | `1e18` | `1e18` | Deploy script
Redemption alpha | `1e18` | `1e18` | Deploy script
Mint minute decay factor | `998076443575628800` | `998076443575628800` | Deploy script
Redemption minute decay factor | `998076443575628800` | `998076443575628800` | Deploy script

Production parameters are not published. Do not copy Sepolia parameters into a mainnet integration unless [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status) and [Contracts & Addresses](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/contracts) later publish them as production values.

---

## Dashboard & Analytics

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app

# Dashboard & Analytics

The **Dashboard** is the app's orientation view. It is where you check balances, open positions, pending rewards, and system health before choosing a transaction tab. The **Analytics** view, covered further down, shows the protocol-wide metrics behind those numbers.

App anchors: **Dashboard** for your own state, and **Analytics** for protocol-wide context. Use both for inspection only; execute actions from the matching task tab after checking a fresh quote.

Image: Polaris Dashboard Earn cards: pUSD and pGOLD Stability Pools, POLAR Staking, and vpETH Staking, each showing APR, deposited or staked amount, and pending gains

*Each Earn card shows its APR, your deposited or staked amount, and the gains accrued so far across each reward token.*

## What to check

Area | What it tells you

**Balances** | WETH, pETH, fpETH, vpETH, pAssets, POLAR, and claimable rewards held by the connected wallet
**Borrow positions** | Open troves, reserve loans, debt, collateral, and health metrics
**Earn positions** | Stability Pool deposits, POLAR Staking balances, and claimable gains
**System status** | Recovery Mode, floor ratio, borrow rates, pool size, and other launch/runtime signals
**Next actions** | Which tabs can adjust or close the position you are viewing

## Read before acting

- A dashboard number is a state read, not a transaction quote.
- A position can change between the Dashboard and the moment your transaction lands.
- Use the transaction tab's quote and simulation before signing.

## Analytics: protocol-wide metrics

Use **Analytics** to inspect protocol-wide state before deciding on a transaction. Analytics is for context and monitoring; action tabs provide the final quote.

Metric | Why it matters

**Floor price** | The contract-defined pETH floor under the bonding-curve assumptions
**Floor ratio** | pETH floor price divided by current pETH market price; frames curve-implied drawdown
**Borrow APR** | Annualized estimate of current pAsset debt cost
**Stability Pool size** | Available first-loss buffer for liquidations
**Recovery Mode status** | Whether a pAsset instance is under system-wide stress restrictions
**PSM/redemption activity** | Peg pressure and fee context for pAsset markets
**POLAR Staking rewards** | Current routed rewards where the staking surface is live

Before relying on a metric:

- Check the timestamp or refresh state before acting.
- Distinguish an APR estimate from realized yield.
- Confirm whether a metric is testnet-only, illustrative, or production-published.
- Use the action tab's current quote for fees, slippage, and final amounts.

## Related docs

- [Borrow](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/borrow) for troves and reserve loans.
- [Earn](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/earn) for Stability Pool and POLAR Staking positions.
- [pETH Floor Price](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price), [Minting pAssets](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting), and [Where the Yield Comes From](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield/yield-sources) for the volatile concepts behind the metrics.

---

## Advanced

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/advanced

# Advanced

Use **Advanced** for tools that expose protocol mechanics more directly than the main task tabs. These surfaces are useful for power users and integrators, but they can bypass the guardrails of a guided flow.

App anchor: **Advanced** tab. Use this surface only when you know the exact contract, function, token, amount, and effect you intend to sign.

## Common Advanced tools

Tool | What it is | Read first

**Approvals** | Token allowances for app contracts | [Safety and Verification](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/safety-verification)
**PSM** | Peg Stability Module actions for a pAsset instance | [Redemptions](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/redemptions-liquidations)
**Reserve Loans** | WETH loans against fpETH floor-value collateral | [pETH Split](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/split)
**POLAR Staking** | Stake, unstake, and claim routed rewards where exposed | [POLAR Tokenomics](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/tokenomics)
**Conversion** | Burn pETH to mint POLAR through the auction | [Conversion Auctions](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/conversion-auctions#how-to-convert)

---

## Borrow

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/borrow

# Borrow

Use **Borrow** for two distinct position types:

App anchor: **Borrow** tab. Trove actions appear as pAsset borrowing/CDP actions; reserve loans appear as fpETH-backed WETH loan actions.

Image: Polaris Borrow tab: pUSD selected, 10 pETH collateral at 75% LTV, with a projected position showing liquidation price, estimated monthly cost, and LTV

*Pick the pAsset to mint, set collateral and LTV, then review the projected position — liquidation price, monthly cost, and LTV — before approving.*

App wording | Docs wording | What it means

**Borrow pAssets** | **Open or adjust a trove** | Deposit pETH collateral and mint pUSD, pGOLD, or another pAsset as debt.
**CDP** | **Trove / CDP instance** | The contract mechanism behind a pAsset borrow position.
**Loan / Reserve Loan** | **Reserve loan** | Borrow WETH against fpETH floor-value collateral; this is not a pAsset trove.

For the full **Borrow / CDP / trove / reserve loan** vocabulary, see [Borrower terminology](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting#borrower-terminology).

## Related docs

- [Open a Polaris Trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/open-a-trove)
- [Managing a Polaris Trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/managing-your-trove)
- [pETH Split: fpETH and vpETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/split)
- [pETH Bonding Curve](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth)

---

## Earn

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/earn

# Earn

Use **Earn** for protocol-native yield surfaces: Stability Pool deposits, reward claims, and the current **POLAR Staking** surface where available.

App anchor: **Earn** tab. Stability Pool deposits and POLAR Staking controls live here when the current deployment exposes them.

Image: Polaris Earn tab: pUSD Stability Pool deposit form at 15.2% APR, with a deposit amount entered and a checkbox to also claim pending pUSD and pETH gains

*Each surface has its own tab; the Stability Pool deposit form shows APR, your balance, and an option to claim pending gains alongside the deposit.*

## Earn surfaces

Surface | What you deposit | What you receive | Read first

**Stability Pool** | pUSD, pGOLD, or another pAsset | Borrower-interest share and liquidation gains | [Stability Pool](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield)
**POLAR Staking** | POLAR | Routed staking rewards where the current deployment exposes them | [POLAR Tokenomics](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/tokenomics)
**vePOLAR locking** | POLAR, once locking is deployed | Stewardship rights and a future yield path | [vePOLAR](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/stewardship/vepolar)

POLAR Staking and vePOLAR are not the same thing. The current staking surface is a stake/unstake/claim flow. vePOLAR locking is a forthcoming vote-escrow mechanism with lock durations, vote-power decay, and stewardship rights.

## Related docs

- [How to deposit to the Stability Pool](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield#how-to-deposit)
- [Where the Yield Comes From](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield/yield-sources)
- [POLAR Tokenomics](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/tokenomics)

---

## Split

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/split

# Split

Use **Split** to divide pETH into two matching legs:

App anchor: **Split** tab. Use it to split pETH into fpETH + vpETH or merge equal fpETH/vpETH balances back into pETH.

Image: Polaris Split tab: 10 pETH splitting into 10 fpETH and 10 vpETH, with a toggle between Split and Merge modes

*Splitting 10 pETH yields 10 fpETH and 10 vpETH; the Merge toggle reverses it when you hold equal balances of both legs.*

```
pETH -> fpETH + vpETH
```

Each unit of pETH split mints one unit of **fpETH** and one unit of **vpETH**. Holding both legs together is economically similar to holding the original pETH, before fees and execution effects. Holding only one leg changes your risk.

## What each leg represents

Token | Role

**fpETH** | The floor-price leg. It can be redeemed through the splitter for WETH at the pETH floor-price value under the contract assumptions. It is also the collateral for reserve loans.
**vpETH** | The premium or volatility leg. It represents exposure to pETH's market value above the floor and can become worth very little if the premium compresses.

For the full mechanism and risks, read [pETH Split: fpETH and vpETH](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/split).

---

## Swap

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/swap

# Swap

Use **Swap** when you want to exchange WETH, pETH, fpETH, vpETH, or a pAsset through the route the app quotes. For pETH, the route may involve the [bonding curve](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth); for pAssets, it may route through app-supported markets or protocol modules.

App anchor: **Swap** tab. Use it for WETH -> pETH, pETH -> WETH, pAsset routes, and any quoted route that shows price impact or slippage before signing.

Image: Polaris Swap tab: selling 5 ETH for pETH, showing the quoted output, minimum received, exchange rate, and a route of WETH through the bonding curve to pETH

*The quote shows output, minimum received, and rate; the route panel makes the path explicit — here WETH through the bonding curve to pETH.*

## Common swaps

Goal | Typical route | Read first

Get pETH | WETH -> pETH | [pETH Bonding Curve](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth)
Exit pETH | pETH -> WETH | [pETH Floor Price](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price)
Get a pAsset to deposit | WETH -> pUSD or pGOLD | [Polaris Asset Catalog](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/passet-catalog)
Rebalance before a debt action | pAsset or pETH swap | [Managing a Trove](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/managing-your-trove)

## Risks

Swapping does not remove pETH price risk, pAsset peg risk, or execution risk. The final amount can differ from the preview because of gas, slippage, MEV, route changes, and transaction ordering.

---

## Zap

URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/zap

# Zap

Use **Zap** when the app bundles several steps into one guided flow. The main yield zap is:

App anchor: **Zap** tab. Treat each bundled step as its own economic action even if the app presents one guided flow.

```
WETH -> pUSD or pGOLD -> Stability Pool deposit
```

The zap is an execution shortcut. It does not change the economics of the underlying swap or deposit.

## What the zap does

1. Takes a WETH amount as input.
2. Quotes the pAsset purchase, such as pUSD or pGOLD.
3. Quotes the Stability Pool deposit for that pAsset.
4. Simulates the bundled operation.
5. Prompts for approvals and transaction signatures.

Depending on the implementation, the app may ask for one transaction or several. Treat every approval and every signature as a separate action.

## Failure modes

A zap can fail because one leg changes before inclusion: slippage, depleted liquidity, stale oracle data, Recovery Mode restrictions, approval mismatch, or gas limits. If it fails after an approval but before the final deposit, inspect your wallet balances before trying again.

## Related docs

- [Swap](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/swap)
- [Earn](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/earn)
- [Stability Pool](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield)
